case_title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. EDWIN L JORDAN et al

docket_entries.description: DISCOVERY 302, MATTER ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR INSPECTION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT EDWIN L. JORDAN IS ORDERED TO SERVE COMPLETE, VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS SERVED ON AUGUST 29, 2014 WITHIN 15 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT EDWIN L. JORDAN IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE ALL DOCUMENTS WITHIN HIS POSSESSION, CUSTODY AND CONTROL THAT ARE RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR INSPECTION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHIN 15 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER VAN ZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED (302).

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 6, 2015 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 302. THE MATTER WAS NOT SET FOR HEARING WITHIN THE TIME PRESCRIBED BY CCP 1005 (MOTION MUST BE SET FOR HEARING NO EARLIER THAN 16 COURT DAYS AFTER FILING + 5 CALENDAR DAYS FOR SERVICE BY MAIL.) PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS ORDERED TO GIVE NOTICE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE TO DEFENDANT VIA OVERNIGHT MAIL NO LATER THAN MARCH 19, 2015 AND FILE A PROOF OF SERVICE WITH THIS COURT INDICATING SUCH SERVICE. Notice Of Motion And Motion Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-06-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brandon Tang telephone number 510-896-9411 for Plaintiff.

No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The matter was discussed. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Motion Terminating Sanctions is granted. Defendant's answer is struck. The Default Clerk is to enter a judgment. Counsel for the Plaintiff is to prepare an order.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-17-2015 Continued To Apr-06-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'s Motion for Terminating Sanctions is continued to April 6, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 302. The matter was not set for hearing within the time prescribed by CCP 1005 (motion must be set for hearing no earlier than 16 court days after filing + 5 calendar days for service by mail.) Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to give notice of the continued hearing date to Defendant via overnight mail no later than March 19, 2015 and file a proof of service with this Court indicating such service. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2015 Set For Trial On Jun-01-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Response To Discovery Order For The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Asserted
Off Calendar Dec-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Caroline McCormick, Esq., 925.628.4470, for plaintiff.
Edwin Jordan, defendant in propria persona.

Matter argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Capital One Bank (USA), N.A.'s Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories and Request for Inspection and Production of Documents and Requests for Admissions Deemed Admitted is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant Edwin L. Jordan is ORDERED to serve complete, verified responses without objection to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories and Request for Admissions served on August 29, 2014 within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on this motion. Defendant Edwin L. Jordan is ORDERED to provide all documents within his possession, custody and control that are responsive to Plaintiff's Request for Inspection and Production of Documents within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on this motion. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem Peter Van Zandt, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported (302).
